For F21/F22/F23, observed LF counts in bins with midpoint above the 90 cm cutoff are set to zero. The 90 cm threshold reproduces the historical treatment documented in WCPFC-SC19-2023/SA-WP-05 for the corresponding Indonesia, Philippines, and Vietnam domestic small-fish length compositions; 90 cm is retained and only bins with midpoint greater than 90 cm are zeroed. Counts are not transferred; LF categories remain in the MFCL option-3 likelihood; MFCL internally renormalizes retained counts; an all-zero LF vector becomes one -1 whole-sample sentinel. Effort creep is not reapplied.

This model belongs to the public 26-model robust-normal design: twelve core age-length/cutoff structures and one targeted N8 structure, each paired as TAGF2OFF and TAGF2ON. Every model uses the complete single-area-derived selectivity baseline, including F29-F33 first-two-age zeros; N8 changes only F12 PS.JP.1 and F13 PL.JP.1. Age-length levels are BASE075, BASE100, REG075, REG100, SUB075, and SUB100. BASE100 is byte-identical to BASE075 except that all 181 effective-sample-size multipliers are 1 rather than 0.75. All models use MFCL option-3 robust-normal LF likelihood with common initial divisor 20; Francis reweighting is intentionally performed only after this initial fit. The processed FRQ already includes the SC22 duplicate-use ISS correction, so no second model-stage /2 is applied. CPUE sigma is retained from Job 5319. TAGF2ON changes only all 98 tag_flags(:,2) values. Fish flag 26=2 evaluates the flag-57 cubic spline on scaled mean length-at-age to produce final selectivity-at-age; flag-61 nodes use that coordinate, while flags 75/3/16 remain age constraints. This setting is separate from the LF likelihood. This model uses age-length level SUB100.

For F21/F22/F23, observed LF counts in bins with midpoint above the 90 cm cutoff are set to zero. The audit reconciles removed counts, affected records, all-zero LF sentinels, and minimum-sample crossings.
